title: Intergenerational Resemblance in Field of Study in the Netherlands
abstract: Level of education has always been the focus of attention in studies on the intergenerational transmission of education. Consequently, we do not know whether eld of study is a relevant new boundary. We expect eld of study to be important, because it indicates the type of resources that students acquire.We tested hypotheses about parental eects on the eld of study of respondents to the Dutch Family Surveys of 1992 and 1998 (N2949). In the Dutch educational system the choice of a specic educational programme is made at various educational levels.The association between the educational elds of father and child appeared to be particularly strong in general, teacher/educational, and agricultural elds, and was hardly aected by other family characteristics. Interestingly, children from lower-class backgrounds were over-represented in elds of study with favourable labour-market opportunities, and children from the economic and cultural elite selected elds where they could reproduce their family capital.
